evals.prompt: I want to optimize my DSPy ticket classifier using VizPy. I have 100 labeled tickets with categories: billing, technical, account, security.
evals.expected_output: A VizPy ContraPromptOptimizer setup for classification with proper vizpy.Score metric, evaluation before/after on a devset, and save step

assertions.name: uses_contra_prompt_optimizer
assertions.description: Uses vizpy.ContraPromptOptimizer (not PromptGradOptimizer) since this is a classification task

assertions.name: uses_vizpy_score_metric
assertions.description: Metric function returns vizpy.Score(value, is_success, feedback) not a plain float or bool

assertions.name: sets_api_key
assertions.description: Sets vizpy.api_key or mentions VIZPY_API_KEY environment variable

assertions.name: includes_dspy_evaluate
assertions.description: Includes dspy.Evaluate on a held-out devset to verify improvement (with float metric wrapper, not vizpy.Score directly)

assertions.name: provider_agnostic_lm
assertions.description: Uses dspy.LM with a generic provider example and alternative comment

assertions.name: saves_optimized_program
assertions.description: Calls optimized.save() to persist the result

evals.prompt: I want to improve my DSPy summarizer. Should I use VizPy or GEPA?
evals.expected_output: A comparison recommending PromptGradOptimizer for generation tasks, with GEPA as the open-source alternative, guidance on metric format differences, and data privacy consideration

assertions.name: mentions_gepa_alternative
assertions.description: Mentions GEPA as the open-source alternative that tunes the same thing (instructions only)

assertions.name: mentions_miprov2
assertions.description: Mentions MIPROv2 if they also want few-shot demo optimization

assertions.name: asks_data_privacy
assertions.description: Asks about or flags data privacy since VizPy sends training data to external servers

assertions.name: correct_metric_format
assertions.description: Notes that VizPy metric must return vizpy.Score, not a plain float like GEPA or MIPROv2

evals.prompt: Set up VizPy to optimize my DSPy program and compare the results with GEPA
evals.expected_output: Side-by-side optimization with VizPy and GEPA, showing the different metric formats and method calls, with evaluation comparing both on the same devset

assertions.name: shows_both_optimizers
assertions.description: Shows both vizpy optimizer (.optimize()) and dspy.GEPA (.compile()) calls

assertions.name: different_metric_formats
assertions.description: Shows that VizPy uses vizpy.Score metric and GEPA uses dict metric — they are not interchangeable

assertions.name: shared_eval_metric
assertions.description: Evaluates both optimized programs on the same devset using a float metric wrapper for dspy.Evaluate

assertions.name: same_optimization_target
assertions.description: Notes that both only tune instructions (same optimization target)

assertions.name: saves_both_programs
assertions.description: Saves both optimized programs separately for comparison
